subject: [PATCH v2 015/110] nilfs2: use PRIino format for i_ino

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the use of PRIino format in error messages for inode numbers, specifically in warning and critical messages. The author acknowledges that the PRIino format should be used consistently throughout the code and agrees to make the necessary changes.

reviews.author: Viacheslav Linux-f2fs-devel
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the PRIino format specifier is not correctly used in nilfs_palloc_commit_free_entry and other functions, leading to potential formatting issues due to the widened i_ino type.
reviews.raw_body:
Reviewed-by: Viacheslav Dubeyko <slava@dubeyko.com>

Thanks,
Slava.

_______________________________________________
Linux-f2fs-devel mailing list
Linux-f2fs-devel@lists.sourceforge.net
https://lists.sourceforge.net/lists/listinfo/linux-f2fs-devel

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch updates the nilfs2 file system to use the PRIino format for i_ino, which is a macro that formats inode numbers as strings. This change prepares the file system for the widening of i_ino from a 32-bit integer to a 64-bit integer via the kino_t type. The patch also updates trace events and printk format strings to match the new field type.
